findings.note: Sandbox check `resolve_within(&cwd, ".")` is a no-op — `.` is always within any directory. Path traversal (e.g. `cwd='/tmp/xxx/../../../etc'`) canonicalizes to `/etc` and the agent escapes the workspace. The only protection is `canonicalize()` failing for nonexistent paths; existing directories outside the workspace are accepted. The contract requires the agent must not escape the workspace root, but `canonicalize()` normalizes rather than rejects.
findings.repro: mkdir -p /tmp/sbx && cargo test --lib acp::server::tests::ac21_session_new_path_traversal_rejected_or_normalized -- --nocapture 2>&1 | grep 'accepted'  # On macOS/Linux where /etc exists, the traversal succeeds and the agent runs in /etc

findings.note: session/new returns stable non-empty sessionId; session/prompt with that sessionId succeeds (no 'session not found' error). Verified by `ac21_session_new_stable_id_valid_for_prompt`.
Nonexistent cwd returns proper INVALID_PARAMS error with descriptive message mentioning cwd. Verified by `ac21_nonexistent_cwd_returns_error`.

session/prompt streams agent_message_chunk with non-empty content delta; final notification is end_turn with valid stopReason (end_turn). Verified by `ac22_session_prompt_streams_chunks_and_end_turn` and `ac22_end_turn_is_last_notification`. Bridge at `src/acp/bridge.rs:225-275` emits buffered partial tokens then end_turn.

Non-text ContentBlock types (resource_link, image) are silently filtered; only text blocks are concatenated and processed. All-non-text prompt returns graceful end_turn with 'Unsupported content types' message (line ~570-585). Server does not crash or hang. Verified by `ac23_mixed_text_and_non_text_no_crash` and `ac23_only_non_text_blocks_returns_graceful`. Minor: user_message_chunk notification for all-non-text path is missing `content` field (line ~567-573).
Sequential session/prompt calls maintain context: second turn response contains 'Alice' and 'stellar' from first turn. stopReason is end_turn. Verified by `ac24_multi_turn_context_preserved`.

stopReason mapping at `handle_session_prompt` line ~670-680: NoMoreToolCalls→"end_turn", Cancelled→"cancelled", BudgetExceeded→"max_turns", Stuck/TranscriptLimit/ProviderStop/PermissionDenialLimit→"error". Bridge at `src/acp/bridge.rs:245-260` has consistent mapping. Case (a) normal completion → end_turn verified. Cases (b) tool-call-finish and (c) max_turns are not testable with current MockProvider (no tool_calls), but the mapping code covers all defined values correctly.

SHA-256 first 12 hex chars (lowercase). Same content ('Hello world') produces identical messageId across two separate sessions. messageId is exactly 12 chars, all lowercase hex [0-9a-f]. Different prompts produce different messageIds. Verified by `ac26_message_id_deterministic_across_sessions` and bridge tests. Known value: 'Hello, world!' → '315f5bdb76d0'.

EventSink in Sprint 2 mode (`tool_call_notifications_enabled: false`) emits only user_message_chunk, agent_message_chunk, and end_turn notifications. No tool_call or tool_call_update emissions. All notifications carry correct sessionId; end_turn has stopReason and turnId. Every notification is well-formed JSON-RPC 2.0 with method='session/update'. Verified by `ac27_event_sink_only_allowed_notifications`.

session/prompt with nonexistent sessionId returns SESSION_NOT_FOUND (-32001) error with descriptive message. Server remains responsive — subsequent session/new succeeds. Verified by `ac29_stale_session_returns_error_not_crash`.

Empty prompt array `[]` returns INVALID_PARAMS error: 'prompt array is empty or contains no text blocks'. Server does not crash, hang, or emit malformed JSON. Verified by `ac210_empty_prompt_array_returns_error_or_helpful`.
